commands.review_summary:
The PR registers `google-vertex` for Google image/video generation and adds Vertex-specific media REST routing, headers, response parsing, and contract tests.

PR surface: Source +199, Tests +17. Total +216 across 10 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current `main` registers only `google` for image/video generation while the runtimes look up the requested `candidate.provider` and fail with the reported missing-provider error for `google-vertex`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are narrow repairs to request-policy/origin handling plus focused tests; the branch still needs refresh or consolidation because GitHub reports it dirty.

target.title: fix(google): enable vertex image and video generation
target.body:
## Summary

Vertex AI generative video currently experiences SDK integration failures when fetching credentials dynamically during the predictLongRunning and polling lifecycle via `gaxios`. This patch safely unblocks video generation by explicitly identifying `google-vertex` requests and routing them to a robust REST fallback path.

- Registers proper runtime capabilities for the `google-vertex` identifier for both image and video generation providers.
- Adds proper project and location resolution for Vertex AI based on environment variables or config.
- Bypasses the `@google/genai` SDK and explicitely routes video `predictLongRunning` correctly through Vertex regional domains instead of AI Studio.
- Updates polling logic to use the `fetchPredictOperation` POST verb mandated by Vertex AI.
- Adapts generated video response parsing to handle Vertex-specific `videos: [{bytesBase64Encoded: ...}]` payload structure.
- Explicitly handles `google-vertex` path building for image generation endpoint (`generateContent`).
- Preserves the `qa-lab` allowlist check by discarding unrelated test changes.

Fixes #90209.

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior or issue addressed:** When a user generates an image or video using the `google-vertex` provider, the request fails because the capability runtime is missing the `google-vertex` identifier. Even if it were present, the video SDK crashes on Vertex ADC and routes incorrectly through AI Studio (`generativelanguage.googleapis.com`) causing `404 Not Found` errors. After the fix, both Vertex image and video endpoints are successfully targeted using their proper regional AI Platform origins and required polling schema.

**Real environment tested:**
- OS: Linux (WSL2 / Ubuntu 24.04)
- Node.js: v26.0.0
- Commit tested: `7568855d65`
- Local OpenClaw workspace: `/home/nyu/openclaw`

**Exact steps or command run after the patch:**
1. Clone the fork and check out the PR branch.
2. Ensure you have valid ADC credentials.
3. Set `GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=us-central1`.
4. Run image generation using the `google-vertex` model.
5. Run video generation using the `google-vertex` model with `OPENCLAW_DEBUG=1` to observe the REST paths.

**Evidence after fix:**

Image generation:
```bash
$ npx tsx src/entry.ts capability image generate --model google-vertex/gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview --prompt "A cute small tuxedo rutin chicken with cyber goggles on an RGB keyboard" --output /tmp/test_image.png

...
image.generate via local
provider: google-vertex
model: gemini-3.1-flash-image-preview
outputs: 1
/tmp/test_image.png

$ file /tmp/test_image.png
/tmp/test_image.png: PNG image data, 1408 x 768, 8-bit/color RGB, non-interlaced
```

Video generation:
```bash
$ GOOGLE_CLOUD_LOCATION=us-central1 OPENCLAW_DEBUG=1 npx tsx src/entry.ts capability video generate --model google-vertex/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001 --prompt "A cute small tuxedo rutin chicken with cyber goggles pecking on an RGB keyboard" --duration 5 --output /tmp/test_video.mp4

...
The project/location from the environment variables will take precedence over the API key from the environment variables.
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:predictLongRunning
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
[DEBUG] requestGoogleVideoJson URL: POST https://us-central1-aiplatform.googleapis.com/v1/projects/nyu-ai-gemini/locations/us-central1/publishers/google/models/veo-3.1-fast-generate-001:fetchPredictOperation
video.generate via local
provider: google-vertex
model: veo-3.1-fast-generate-001
outputs: 1
/tmp/test_video.mp4

$ file /tmp/test_video.mp4
/tmp/test_video.mp4: ISO Media, MP4 Base Media v1 [ISO 14496-12:2003]
```

**Observed result after the fix:** The CLI successfully routes both `google-vertex` image and video payloads via `https://<location>-aiplatform.googleapis.com`. The `predictLongRunning` job initiates properly, and the polling mechanism queries using the `fetchPredictOperation` POST endpoint until completion, avoiding the `gaxios` runtime crash and downloading a valid MP4/PNG.

**Not tested:**
- External proxy deployments of this fix.
target.author: neogeosnk
